text:
Assign the following document to exactly one of these labels (copy the label text exactly):
- Arts_and_Entertainment
- Law_and_Government
- News
- People_and_Society
- Sensitive_Subjects
- Sports
- Travel_and_Transportation

Here are labeled examples from the training set:
---
Document:
A decision to withdraw may be made this week.
Washington (AFP): The nuclear deal with Iran is dangerous for America. A resolution will also be presented along with the announcement of the decision, through which Iran can prove that it is far from developing nuclear weapons. This was stated by US President Donald Trump in an interview with a news channel.
Trump said that Iran's nuclear deal with major powers in 2015 is a waste agreement. We can achieve nothing by running on it. A total of six major countries, including the United States, signed the agreement with Iran. Under the agreement, Iran has pledged not to build nuclear weapons, in return for which America and Europe have lifted sanctions. Also, Iran is being given technical assistance. Trump has been opposed to this agreement from the beginning. After Iran's recent medium-range missile test, the US has sent a resolution to Parliament to re-impose sanctions on Iran. According to procedure, the US President has to tell his parliament every 90 days whether Iran is complying with the 2015 agreement and whether the decision to lift sanctions should remain in force. Trump has so far recommended staying in the agreement, but he will have to send his recommendation to Parliament once again next Sunday. Trump said his latest recommendation could be important for the agreement. If they recommend not continuing the agreement, they will first take the opinion of their advisors, friendly European countries and the International Atomic Energy Agency.
To see the latest news, photos, videos and live scores on mobile, go to m.jagran.com
Tags:
Web Title: iran atomic accord

Label: News
---
Document:
With the aim of promoting Azerbaijan and ensuring objective information about our country to the opposite side, a music disc titled "Pearls of Azerbaijani Music" has been released in Tashkent through the project of the Heydar Aliyev Azerbaijani Culture Center under the Embassy of Azerbaijan in Uzbekistan.
It should be noted that music lovers in Uzbekistan have repeatedly applied to the Azerbaijani Culture Center expressing their desire to obtain songs about Azerbaijani music.
Precisely taking into account the great interest in Azerbaijani music, the Azerbaijani Culture Center selected the pearls of our national music and released them in disc form with the purpose of promoting them in the brotherly country.
The music disc titled "Pearls of Azerbaijani Music" includes both masterpieces of Azerbaijani classical music such as Uzeyir Hajibayli's "Koroglu Overture," "Ashig Style," "Arshin Mal Alan - Girls' Chorus," Fikret Amirov's "I Will Look For You," "Kurda Ovshari," Kara Karayev's "On Stormy Roads," "Seven Beauties," as well as hits considered masterpieces of Azerbaijani music of the 20th century, such as Muslim Magomayev's "Azerbaijan," "Without You," Rashid Behbudov's "Oh Lachin," "My Beautiful Beloved Came To My Mind," "Mother," Shovkat Alekberova's "My Azerbaijan," "Evening," Zeynab Khanlarova's "What Was Left To Whom," "My Youth," "You Are Mine Karabakh," and so on.
Stars of modern Azerbaijani music and beloved songs also found their reflection on the disc. Thus, Alim Gasimov's "Dear Friend," "Memories," Akif Islamzadeh's "Dark Eyed One," Flora Karimova's "Darling," "I Miss You Shusha," Azeri's "The Black Sea Was Thrashing," Elnare Abdullayeva's "My Azerbaijan," Miralam Miralamov's "Without You" were included in the series of musical pearls and presented on the disc.
We would like to inform you that cultural centers operating at Nizami State Pedagogical University of Tashkent, the Uzbek State Conservatory, and Samarkand State University have been established within the framework of the project of the Cultural Center under the Embassy. In the near future, Azerbaijani corners will be created at Alisher Navoi State Uzbek Language and Literature University and the Uzbek State University of Culture and Arts. Numerous books, photos and paintings, maps, Azerbaijani feature films, video clips, documentaries are gifted to these corners for the promotion of our country.
It should be noted that the music discs were produced in limited quantities and will only be donated to the Uzbek State Conservatory, music and art schools, the established Azerbaijani corners, as well as individuals who showed interest in our national music during the presentation ceremony of the music disc.

Label: Arts_and_Entertainment
---
Document:
The Public Services Regulatory Commission has initiated a process to review electricity tariffs on its own initiative, without waiting for applications from energy system companies.
The Chairman of the Public Services Regulatory Commission, Robert Nazaryan, clarified at today's meeting with journalists that Armenia's energy system companies had long wanted to raise tariffs, but were waiting for the issue of gas price increase to be resolved.
“Why on our initiative at this moment? Because there were already written requests from them, we had analyses, the picture is clear to us,” Nazaryan detailed. – “We have timely submitted applications from all companies operating in the electrical system, which were at our disposal. They had long wanted to raise electricity prices, but they were waiting because there was a problem due to the increase in gas prices.”
Since April 1, 2009, the population of Armenia pays 30 drams during the day and 20 drams at night for 1 kWh of electricity.
Recall that yesterday HayRusGasArd company applied to the Public Services Regulatory Commission proposing to increase the gas tariff by about 67 percent, making the current 132 thousand drams per thousand cubic meters 221 thousand drams.
Economists do not consider it accidental that discussions about raising both gas and electricity prices began after the elections.
"When you go to elections, you sell your vote, you must also be prepared for the consequences," economist Armenak Chatinyan commented in an interview with Azatutyun Radio.
In turn, economist Samvel Avagyan insists that the Government has the opportunity not to raise electricity prices.
“I believe that it is possible not to revise the price of electricity, because gas does not play a serious role in our energy balance today. Maybe there is another factor we don't know, maybe the raw material for the nuclear power plant has become more expensive?” said Avagyan.
Economists believe that raising gas and electricity prices will create a basis for a serious social explosion.

Label: Law_and_Government
---
Document to classify:
Holiday home Apartments Ypres, Ypres is located in the historical center of the city of Ypres, close to the Grand Place; In Flanders Fields Museum; Ypres Museum and Tourist Office. The house is within walking distance of the Menin Gate where the Last Post is sounded every evening at 8:00 PM.
The holiday home contains 3 comfortable apartments.
"BELFRY" on the 2nd floor is a very cozy apartment for 2 people with bedroom double bed. The bathroom has a bath with shower. Belfry is stylishly decorated so you can relax in the lively city of Ypres.
"CATHEDRAL" apartment on the 1st floor is very comfortable for 3 adults or for a family with children. There is 1 bedroom with a double bed and a separate sleeping area with bunk beds. A bath with shower is ready for you after a pleasant walk on the ramparts or other activity.
In the holiday home both apartments have a separate kitchen with all modern cooking appliances, air conditioning, wireless internet and digital television.
"BELLS" on the ground floor, a nice apartment with 2 separate beds and kitchenette. Separate bathroom with shower and sink.
Enjoyment can be found in the spacious patio where peace and coziness are assets.
The warm baker next to the holiday home provides a delicious breakfast. A large supermarket for all purchases is within walking distance.
A closed garage at the apartment is available upon request or a garage box 150 meters from the apartments.
Ypres is a city that will certainly appeal to you. The numerous places and museums to discover. The Grand Place with cozy cafes and tasty restaurants to taste.
In the Flanders Fields Museum and in the Ypres Museum you experience the history of World War I in Flanders. Since 1928, the Last Post has been sounded every evening at 8 o'clock.
A visit to the Cloth Hall, Saint Martin's Cathedral, Rijsel Gate and the ramparts is a must.
Near Ypres is the Hooge Crater Museum in a restored church.
www.tourism Ypres.
In Zonnebeke you can visit the Memorial Museum Passendale and the Tyne Cot Cemetery.
Cycling and hiking in the Westhoek to fully discover Ypres.
For the children there is Bellewaerde Park with the brand new Aquapark.
You will definitely want to come back to Apartments Ypres...
More information via Tourism Ypres
Belfry Apartment
Description
The Belfry apartment is a very quiet and pleasant stay in the historic city center of Ypres. All facilities are available. Shops and restaurants are within walking distance of the Belfry apartment. We wish you a pleasant stay with us.
Number of persons 2
Cathedral Apartment
Description
The Cathedral sleeps 4 people. The tastefully decorated apartment offers views of St. Martin's Cathedral in Ypres. There are 2 bedrooms. One bedroom with a double bed and one bedroom with a bunk bed. The kitchen is equipped with all modern appliances. A bathtub with shower allows you to rest after an activity.
Number of persons 4

Answer with one label from the list above only.
